title: Couepia schottii
taxonomy: Couepia schottii is a species of plant within the family Rubiaceae. This family is one of the largest and most diverse in the angiosperm lineage. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the madder family, comprises over 13,000 known species. Couepia schottii is a part of this extensive family. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of the plant with other species within the Rubiaceae family.

Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal potential of Couepia schottii.

intro: Couepia schottii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Chrysophyllaceae and commonly known as "Couepia" or "Cupiá". The health benefits of Couepia schottii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
